source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
According to the Weinberg-Salam model, the Higgs boson $\phi$ couples to every elementary fermion $f$ ( $f$ may be a quark or lepton) in the form
$$
\frac{e m_{f}}{m_{W}} \phi \bar{f} f,
$$
where $m_{f}$ is the mass of the fermion $f, e$ is the charge of the electron, and $m_{W}$ is the mass of the $W$ boson. Some theorists believe that the Higgs boson weighs approximately 10 GeV . If so do you believe it would be observed (in practice) as a resonance in $e^{+} e^{-}$ annihilation (Fig. 3.22)? Roughly how large would the signal to background ratio be at resonance?
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string rather than a concrete file path or URL
question explicitly references Fig. 3.22 and record marks vision_relevance as essential

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object string rather than a concrete file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干显式提到“Fig. 3.22”，并询问在共振处的 signal-to-background ratio，背景量级很可能需要结合图中过程/曲线判断。
输入字段给出 requires_image=true 且 text_dominant=false，说明上游已将该题标记为非纯文本主导。
虽然题干提供了耦合公式，但是否“in practice”可观测仍带有图文联合判断成分，因此图像依赖偏高而非满分。
multi_step: 需要从 Higgs-fermion 耦合公式推出对电子的耦合强度，并进一步估计 e+e- 湮灭产生 Higgs 的共振信号强度。
还需要将信号与背景过程进行比较，得到 resonance 处的 signal-to-background ratio，至少包含两步以上物理推断/数量级估算。
答案是具体数值而非直接读图，表明不是单一步骤提取。
verifiability: answer_type=short_text，标准答案给出明确数值“1.5 × 10^-4”，便于后续做数值匹配或容差核验。
题目目标是一个无量纲比值，输出形式清晰，较适合规则化验证。
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true，且答案与题干文本均存在，基础核验条件较好。

risk_flags: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object string rather than a concrete file path or URL

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和主图像资产都存在，核心语义支撑完整，不属于信息缺失样本。
初步评分较高：图像依赖、多步推理和可验证性分别为 0.82、0.74、0.90，具备进入候选池的价值。
已知问题仅是 metadata.image_paths 记录格式异常，但 image_manifest 中已有可用实际图片路径，且 registry_passed=true，风险可控。

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text:
According to the Weinberg-Salam model, the Higgs boson $\phi$ couples to every elementary fermion $f$ ( $f$ may be a quark or lepton) in the form
$$
\frac{e m_{f}}{m_{W}} \phi \bar{f} f,
$$
where $m_{f}$ is the mass of the fermion $f, e$ is the charge of the electron, and $m_{W}$ is the mass of the $W$ boson. Some theorists believe that the Higgs boson weighs approximately 10 GeV . If so do you believe it would be observed (in practice) as a resonance in $e^{+} e^{-}$ annihilation (Fig. 3.22)? Roughly how large would the signal to background ratio be at resonance?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string rather than a concrete file path or URL
question explicitly references Fig. 3.22 and record marks vision_relevance as essential
metadata.reasoning_chain:
The process $e^{+} e^{-} \rightarrow \bar{f} f$ consists of the following interactions:
$$
e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{\gamma, Z^{0}} \bar{f} f \quad \text { and } \quad e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{H} \bar{f} f .
$$

When $\sqrt{S}=10 \mathrm{GeV}, Z^{0}$ exchange can be ignored. Consider $e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{\gamma} \bar{f} f$. The total cross section is given approximately by
$$
\sigma_{\bar{f} f} \approx \frac{4 \pi \alpha^{2}}{3 S} Q_{f}^{2},
$$
where $\alpha$ is the fine structure constant and $Q_{f}$ is the charge (in units of the electron charge) of the fermion. Thus
$$
\sigma\left({ }^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{\gamma} \bar{f} f\right)=\frac{4 \pi \alpha^{2}}{3 S} \sum Q_{f}^{2} \cdot a_{f},
$$
where $a_{f}=1$ for lepton, $a_{f}=3$ for quark. As $\Sigma Q_{f}^{2} a_{f}=\left(\frac{1}{9}+\frac{4}{9}+\frac{1}{9}\right.$ $\left.+\frac{4}{9}+\frac{1}{9}\right) \times 3+1+1+1=\frac{20}{3}, S=m_{H}^{2}$, we have
$$
\sigma\left(e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{\gamma} \bar{f} f\right)=\frac{4 \pi \alpha^{2}}{3 S} \frac{20}{3} \approx 8 \pi \alpha^{2} / S=\frac{8 \pi \alpha^{3}}{m_{H}^{2}} .
$$

For the $e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{H} \bar{f} f$ process we have at resonance $\left(J_{H}=0\right)$
$$
\sigma\left(e^{+} e^{-
\xrightarrow{H}} \bar{f} f\right)=\pi \lambda^{2} \Gamma_{e e} / \Gamma \approx \pi p^{*-2} \Gamma_{e e} / \Gamma .
$$

As a rough estimate, taking $\Gamma_{e e} \approx m^{2}$, i.e., $\Gamma_{e e} / \Gamma \approx\left(0.5 \times 10^{-3}\right)^{2} / 15.3 \approx$ $1.6 \times 10^{-8}$ and $p^{* 2}=\frac{m_{H}^{2}}{4}$, we have
$$
\begin{aligned}
\sigma\left(e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{H} \bar{f} f\right): \sigma\left(e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{\gamma} \bar{f} f\right) & =\left(\frac{4 \pi}{m_{H}^{2}} 1.6 \times 10^{-8}\right)\left(\frac{8 \pi \alpha^{2}}{m_{H}^{2}}\right)^{-1} 
& \approx 0.8 \times 10^{-8} / \alpha^{2} \approx 1.5 \times 10^{-4} .
\end{aligned}
$$In $e^{+} e^{-}$annihilation in the 10 GeV region, the background, which is mainly due to the photon process, is almost $10^{4}$ times as strong as the $H_{0}$ resonance process. The detection of the latter is all but impossible.

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain:
The process $e^{+} e^{-} \rightarrow \bar{f} f$ consists of the following interactions:
$$
e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{\gamma, Z^{0}} \bar{f} f \quad \text { and } \quad e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{H} \bar{f} f .
$$

When $\sqrt{S}=10 \mathrm{GeV}, Z^{0}$ exchange can be ignored. Consider $e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{\gamma} \bar{f} f$. The total cross section is given approximately by
$$
\sigma_{\bar{f} f} \approx \frac{4 \pi \alpha^{2}}{3 S} Q_{f}^{2},
$$
where $\alpha$ is the fine structure constant and $Q_{f}$ is the charge (in units of the electron charge) of the fermion. Thus
$$
\sigma\left({ }^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{\gamma} \bar{f} f\right)=\frac{4 \pi \alpha^{2}}{3 S} \sum Q_{f}^{2} \cdot a_{f},
$$
where $a_{f}=1$ for lepton, $a_{f}=3$ for quark. As $\Sigma Q_{f}^{2} a_{f}=\left(\frac{1}{9}+\frac{4}{9}+\frac{1}{9}\right.$ $\left.+\frac{4}{9}+\frac{1}{9}\right) \times 3+1+1+1=\frac{20}{3}, S=m_{H}^{2}$, we have
$$
\sigma\left(e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{\gamma} \bar{f} f\right)=\frac{4 \pi \alpha^{2}}{3 S} \frac{20}{3} \approx 8 \pi \alpha^{2} / S=\frac{8 \pi \alpha^{3}}{m_{H}^{2}} .
$$

For the $e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{H} \bar{f} f$ process we have at resonance $\left(J_{H}=0\right)$
$$
\sigma\left(e^{+} e^{-
\xrightarrow{H}} \bar{f} f\right)=\pi \lambda^{2} \Gamma_{e e} / \Gamma \approx \pi p^{*-2} \Gamma_{e e} / \Gamma .

$$
\begin{aligned}
\sigma\left(e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{H} \bar{f} f\right): \sigma\left(e^{+} e^{-} \xrightarrow{\gamma} \bar{f} f\right) & =\left(\frac{4 \pi}{m_{H}^{2}} 1.6 \times 10^{-8}\right)\left(\frac{8 \pi \alpha^{2}}{m_{H}^{2}}\right)^{-1} 
& \approx 0.8 \times 10^{-8} / \alpha^{2} \approx 1.5 \times 10^{-4} .
\end{aligned}
$$In $e^{+} e^{-}$annihilation in the 10 GeV region, the background, which is mainly due to the photon process, is almost $10^{4}$ times as strong as the $H_{0}$ resonance process. The detection of the latter is all but impossible.
